transcript.text: Hey Mike, this meeting is being recorded.
Still in Amsterdam?
Yes sir.
Back stateside.
Oh man, not till September 8th.
Yeah, AppSignals in Amsterdam and they were like the biggest sponsor at this conference next week.
So, here doing work and then next week, booth duty.
Nice.
I've been to my fair share of conferences on the sales side and on the sales side, those are draining.
It's like you're in demo mode for five hours straight.
Amsterdam's on the bucket list though, never been.
It's pretty cool.
I don't know.
There's canals.
It's very clean.
It's a cool.
Live, it's a chill, it's a chill European city.
I don't know, you'd need to spend more than like two days here though, you know, it's smaller than you think in terms of like that, where the action is and the touristy stuff.
Yeah, I've heard it's very walkable, like a good place to ride a bike as well.
Oh yeah, I rent a bike, I rented a bike, it's the best.
That's awesome.
Well, thanks for jumping on.
Yeah, you bet.
Figured it'd be good for us to do like a quick connect so I could go into the platform, like show you under the hood with our workflows.
And then for our next meeting, we can bring Marcel in and you'll basically be ready to ask like those super detailed or tough questions and determine if GrowthX is the right fit for AppSignal.
Sound good for today?
Yeah, that sounds good.
Let's do it.
Okay, cool.
From the last combo, just some quick...
Things I remember for some good context setting, you mentioned that like, you know, AppSignal, the superpower is content, but there's a small percent that turn into signups.
It's not very strategic, right?
And you guys have like a nice niche as being 80% of Datadog for 20% of the price.
But at the same time, ATM is crowded.

So, you know, anything that would allow you to scale content with a strategic focus and not have to, you know, go into a tool like CareOps and build all these workflows yourself would really help out with some of the work you're doing.
That's right.
And we value as a company, creativity outside the box stuff.
So we have a, we have a fun factor or a, or a willingness to experiment point score for us as the client.
Yeah, that definitely gets you bonus points with Marcel.
He loves experimentation and getting fun with some of the content that's created outside of just the stuff that's going to do well with SEO and GEO.
So I'll show you a little behind the hood here.
You've got obviously like your laptop where you can see me screen share.
Yep, I can.
I'm in my hotel room.
I'm just not showing video because it's a long day and I just don't want to.
I wouldn't either.
You're also recording this too.
Do you mind if I can get a copy specifically this portion?
Yeah, 100% would be more than happy to do that.
Okay, sweet, sweet.
So I might want to share this around.
Yeah, so the big thing with us is that we start with the eight-week strategy sprint.
That we can really nail down the context engineering with what...
We put into our workflows and also be on the same page about like the topic clusters that we want to target.

So, you know, making sure that we've got the personas that you want to consume this content clearly identified, calibrating it to your voice and your brand, setting those writing guidelines, and then defining topics where there's opportunities between the search volume and the ability to rank, where we can get quick wins.
So to look at the artifacts, there's the company context that we're working on, and we can do a lot of this by just ingesting different documentation or recordings.
Sorry.
Go ahead.
Sorry.
My Alexa just, or not my Alexa, my Siri on my laptop just started talking.
Oh, no worries.
Sorry.
Oh, worries.
worries.
Oh, Oh, Oh, no
All all good.
So the company context, we're going super deep here into like your elevator pitch, your ideal customers, verticals, and personas.
And then we'll even develop artifacts that could be like industry specific if that's helpful.
So for engine and the travel booking space.
Are these takeaway artifacts or like if we leave growthx, you're keeping them?
You guys would be able to take these with you if you have growthx.
Yeah, we're not going to hold anything hostage.
But this is just like really getting inside the brain of the folks you're trying to go after.
Fears and uncertainties, motivations, how to win their trust, lose their trust, what they care about.
And then once we've got all the artifacts ready to go, we'll start working through these pipelines where we'll create the ascent or create the assignments and create the articles.
So for engine, like in the strategy,
At a G-Sprint, we finished that up with them, but by like week four, week five, we had already published like 30 different articles.

We'll keep this content OS, we'll work with you to set up a review cadence, and then at a certain point, we will just write, like, you'll feel comfortable enough that we'll just be able to publish autonomously for you.
So I want to give you a quick example of like an article that we're working on for Airbyte, and you can see all these different styles.

Steps, where we'll create a brief, we'll do deep research, we'll create a draft of the article, and what's great about this is that with our experts in the loop, they've got the ability to refine this content with the context of all the artifacts included.
So they can directly edit this, it's not going to be completely AI generated, and under the hood with each of these steps, you'll see that we have the workflow layer where each step is built in code.
So I could go into it.
So the brief step here, and you would see that like each step is hundreds of sub-steps.

We are starting with the keyword traffic, fetching organic search results, analyzing those results, looking at competitor content, and then using all of that to inform the brief that you see here, right, which is really detailed.

From there, if we looked at like the research that we do or the fact-checking that we do, on the research step, we're generating research questions and then performing deep research, validating that against different third-party sources so that you can have, right, like well-researched content.
The whole idea with what we're doing is that we want to make sure our workflows include all the most important factors from a GEO perspective.
So that's the comprehensiveness, the freshness.
There's a lot of refresh opportunities that companies will find working with us.

And then we'll even, you know, continue on to like the second layer where we'll split the entire article into chunks, extract passages, and then do additional research to validate like the accuracy of each passage.
If there is not enough evidence in terms of like the confidence interval that the passage in the article is accurate, we'll actually go in here and recommend a rewrite.
And the editor will have the opportunity.
These are all high confidence intervals.
Do you have clients that have their own humans in the loop?
Not in the platform today, since it's just internal, but we are planning to open up the platform to clients over the next like two to four months.
And it could even be sooner.
Good thing to follow up with Marcel on in the next call.
But, you know, the client is definitely.
Like reviewing what we've prepared.
That would be the equivalent.
And can be as involved or uninvolved as you want to be.
Yeah.
I just ask because today, if you remember from the first conversation, we spend $4,000 to $6,000 a month.
If you go to our blog, all of those are external authors, every single one, except for our own product announcements.
And there are various experts on certain programming languages, et cetera.
And I was just thinking, oh, well, we have experts.
I wonder how we can take advantage of them here or not.
That was just sort of the thought.
No, I love the context because with Biologica, which is like this very early startup, CEO of Allbirds created this company in the women's supplement space.
We had a step where like.

Their chief medical officer is doing a review, but we built a custom workflow for them where we had done some interviews with the chief medical officer where we could have an artifact that is basically feeding in all the expertise that we learned so that at a certain point, it stopped being a manual step where the chief medical officer would review what we had written, and it became a part of the actual workflow itself.

So we could do something similar like that with some of the experts that you guys have in-house so that they're almost synthetically in the workflow as a review step when the content's being generated.
Okay.
Thanks for addressing that.
Yeah, no problem.
And then here's like a good example, right, where the confidence interval was low, and we took the passage, found some additional research here about how the number was low.
More than 300, and then, right, did, like, the rewrite with the evidence of why that rewrite was made.

And the editor, like, has the ability to, like, approve or reject these, so it's not just, like, blindly approving any scenario where there's a difference, right, between what was written and what was researched after the fact.

So it's just, like, a look under the hood at some of the things that we're doing, and this is one that's even generating, like, infographics for the article and then publishing, like, directly in the CMS.
And so that's just, like, a look at how fast we can go.

And then I think what I wanted to end on here for you today and just this kind of quick demo is, you this piece from Augment Code, we wrote this on July 29th, and we do, like, LLM visibility tracking.
As well, so we saw this start to show up in a lot of different chat GPT prompts within a matter of days.
But even, like, the...

Beyond that, what's great about this piece right here is like, you know, I searched AI enterprise coding tools and you started to see like augment, I mean, our article, right, is generating as the first citation in AI mode.
But they're also like recommending augment code against like GitHub and Cursor and other tools.
Um, so we've got a lot of great examples of clients who we've had very quick wins and results on the organic side.
And, um, I'm excited to, you know, dig deeper and let you reconnect with Marcel the next time we chat.
Sweet.
Yeah, thank you for this.
Definitely share it.
This gives me a better understanding of the process, the workflow and the behind the scenes.
So this was definitely helpful.
And we'll figure out schedules when I get back in town.
Sounds good.
I don't want to, yeah, next week is about the conference, and also next week is launch week.
It's like a whole thing.
So I'm just picking back up projects when I get back to San Francisco.
No problem.

I think my only, you know, I'm just going to be candid with you, like our likelihood of close, speaking sales terms, it's still early stages, and the opportunity, likelihood of close is, God, I don't know, 20%, 30%, and our biggest, you know, resistance is price.
We're price sensitive.
We're not a big VC-funded company.
However, we are already spending four to six grand a month on humans writing content.
And AI is a giant initiative within the.
The company, even from like our, we're more or less like private equity growth.
They own like 60% of the company and it's a huge initiative for them.
So anyways, I'm just sort of thinking in my head, like the PR spin and how to sell it internally, which is a good sign for you.
But the ethos is, well, let's see if we could build this ourselves with other tools.
And I think I would say with limited capacity, I just don't want to.
And the value exchange here compared to the build versus buy, I think nets out the same.
And then the quality component is much higher.
So thinking out loud, giving you the honest take, the CEO and my counterparts in the leadership team.
Again, I'm...
It's fractional, but I have a ton of exploration.
They trust me.
They know that I'm evaluating options here.
Yeah.
But I definitely need to be ready to be like, here's why we don't build it ourselves as well as here's why we should build it ourselves, which is something I'm actually thinking.
Well, can we not just roll this ourselves and see what that might be like?
so take that.
You don't necessarily need to respond to it now.
I'm still very interested.
I'm just giving you the like, a ton of context to help you as a seller here and where this thing might go next.
Yeah, from one Texas guy to another, really appreciate that.
I would say, you know, don't feel like you need to make your decision until that meeting with Marcel.
Marcel.
Marcel.
So,
Because four weeks into the job, I'm just like nowhere near to where he is with the ability to articulate everything on the build and, you know, try to do it yourself option.

And if you can, Mike, like would recommend like bring the CEO or, you know, someone else from the leadership team to the meeting with Marcel because he has this uncanny ability to go super deep into like the growthx difference, as you know.
So I'll work on scheduling that with you after conference week and you probably will have everything you need to know to make your decision after that meeting.
Amazing.
Thanks so much for the time today.
You bet.
Enjoy Amsterdam.
See you later.
Talk soon.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Follow-up demo of GrowthX platform for AppSignal, focusing on workflows and content strategy capabilities.](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=91.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX offers an 8-week strategy sprint to nail down context engineering and topic clusters](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=226.0)
  - [Platform includes detailed workflows for content creation, research, and fact-checking](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=390.0)
  - [AppSignal spends $4-6k/month on external authors; GrowthX could potentially integrate their experts into the workflow](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
  - [Mike (AppSignal) estimates 20-30% likelihood of close, with price sensitivity being the main concern](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=799.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX Platform Overview

  - [8-week strategy sprint to establish context, personas, and topic clusters](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=226.0)
  - [Detailed company context artifacts (elevator pitch, ideal customers, verticals, personas)](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=271.0)
  - [Content creation pipelines with multiple steps (brief, research, drafting, editing)](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=390.0)
  - [Workflow layer with hundreds of sub-steps for each content creation phase](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [AI-assisted content generation with human experts in the loop for refinement](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=390.0)
  - [Fact-checking and research validation built into the workflow](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)

### Content Quality and SEO Focus

  - [Workflows incorporate key SEO factors (comprehensiveness, freshness)](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=461.0)
  - [Automatic fact-checking with confidence intervals for passage accuracy](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)
  - [Ability to generate infographics and publish directly to CMS](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=681.0)
  - [LLM visibility tracking to monitor content performance in AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT)](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=698.0)
  - [Quick wins in organic search results (example: Augment Code article ranking well)](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=718.0)

### Client Involvement and Customization

  - [Clients can be as involved or uninvolved in the review process as desired](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
  - [Plans to open up the platform to clients in 2-4 months](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
  - [Potential for custom workflows to integrate client's in-house experts (e.g., Biologica case study)](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)

### AppSignal's Current Content Strategy

  - [$4,000-$6,000 monthly spend on external authors for blog content](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
  - [All blog posts, except product announcements, written by external experts](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=556.0)
  - [Interest in potentially leveraging existing experts within the GrowthX workflow](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=572.0)

### Pricing and Decision Factors

  - [AppSignal is price-sensitive, not heavily VC-funded](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=825.0)
  - [AI is a major initiative for AppSignal and their private equity growth owners](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=837.0)
  - [Internal discussions on build vs. buy, with capacity limitations favoring buying](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=872.0)
  - [Need to justify why not to build in-house and the value proposition of GrowthX](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=891.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Schedule a meeting with Marcel (GrowthX) after Mike's conference week](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=993.0)
  - [Mike to consider bringing AppSignal's CEO or other leadership to the Marcel meeting](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=974.0)
  - [Tyler to share the recording of the demo portion with Mike](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=763.0)
  - [Mike to further evaluate the build vs. buy option and prepare questions for the next meeting](https://fathom.video/share/NKzbEsszqpxBmLy57QP9s9wD26rnS-KH?tab=summary&timestamp=922.0)

action_items.description: Send Mike recording of demo portion of meeting
Schedule follow-up meeting w/ Mike & Marcel after Mike's conference week
